name: Castillo de Chillón — circular desde Chillón
region_intro: Hiking trails around Chillón are characterized by a rugged, dry environment with landscapes shaped by Holocene-age pediments and low stream terraces. The terrain often features very gravelly alluvium derived from igneous rocks, contributing to its distinct character. This semi-bolson landscape offers a unique setting for outdoor activities, with elevations ranging significantly. The area's historical mining heritage also adds a cultural dimension to many routes. Best hiking trails around Chillón The most popular hiking route is Ascent to the Castillo de Chillón — circular through the Parque Minero de Almadén, a 6.2 miles (9.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 56 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the historical Almadén Mining Park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castillo de Chillón — circular desde Chillón, a moderate 6.1 miles (9.8 km) path. This circular route offers views of the Castillo de Chillón and the surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the Ascent to Cerro de San Blas — round trip from Baterno, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 31 minutes. Hiking around Chillón is defined by its dry, rugged environment, historical mining landscapes, and gravelly terrains. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Chillón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 15 hikers have used komoot to explore Chillón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Chillón?
A: There are 12 hiking routes available in Chillón, offering a variety of experiences from easy strolls to more challenging treks through the region's unique landscape.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Chillón?
A: The terrain around Chillón is characterized by a rugged, dry environment with landscapes shaped by Holocene-age pediments and low stream terraces. You'll often encounter very gravelly alluvium derived from igneous rocks, creating a distinct semi-bolson landscape. Elevations can vary significantly, offering diverse views.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families in Chillón?
A: Yes, Chillón offers 4 easy hiking routes. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1268742662/ascent-to-cerro-de-san-blas-round-trip-from-baterno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Cerro de San Blas — round trip from Baterno&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.0 km long and takes about 1 hour 31 minutes to complete. Another easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197260438/miners-trail-stage-1" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miners' Trail - Stage 1&lt;/a&gt;, at 4.9 km.

Q: Can I find challenging or advanced hiking routes in Chillón?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, there is 1 difficult route available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284099752/short-route-trail-el-valle-y-las-hectareas-circular-from-alamillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Short Route Trail "El Valle y las Hectáreas" — circular from Alamillo&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 21.6 km trail that typically takes around 5 hours 48 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Chillón?
A: Yes, many routes in Chillón are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197289964/ascent-to-the-castillo-de-chillon-circular-through-the-parque-minero-de-almaden" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Castillo de Chillón — circular through the Parque Minero de Almadén&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular trail exploring the historical mining park.

Q: What are some notable historical or cultural sites I can explore while hiking in Chillón?
A: The Chillón area is rich in historical mining heritage. You can explore sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3901719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén Mining Park&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3901752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miners' Museum&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5236773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saint Mary of the Star&lt;/a&gt;. Many trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197260438/miners-trail-stage-1" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miners' Trail - Stage 1&lt;/a&gt;, directly connect you with this unique history.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Chillón dog-friendly?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on each trail can vary, generally, trails in natural areas like Chillón are accessible for dogs on a leash. It's always recommended to check local signage and be prepared with water, especially given the dry environment.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Chillón?
A: The hiking routes in Chillón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Hikers often praise the unique historical mining landscapes and the rugged, dry environment that offers a distinct outdoor experience.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Chillón?
A: While specific parking information for every trailhead is not detailed, routes often start from or pass through towns like Chillón or Alamillo, where parking facilities are typically available. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route for the most convenient parking options.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Chillón?
A: Given the region's 'ustic aridic soil moisture regime,' indicating a dry environment, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant times for hiking in Chillón. Temperatures are milder, making for more comfortable conditions than the heat of summer. Winter hiking is also an option, though conditions can be cooler.

Q: Are there any specific attractions related to the Almadén Mining Park that I can visit?
A: Absolutely. The Almadén Mining Park is a central feature of the region. Besides the park itself, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3901752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miners' Museum&lt;/a&gt; to delve deeper into the area's rich mining history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5906229" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;town of Almadén&lt;/a&gt; itself is also a significant historical site.

Q: What is the longest hiking trail available in Chillón?
A: The longest hiking trail in Chillón is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284099752/short-route-trail-el-valle-y-las-hectareas-circular-from-alamillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Short Route Trail "El Valle y las Hectáreas" — circular from Alamillo&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 21.6 kilometers and is classified as difficult.
tips: Small town near Almadén, integrated into the Valle de Alcudia region. Its most emblematic building is the Church of San Juan Bautista and Santo Domingo de Silo, which dates back to the 16th century. A few kilometers from the town, in the Sierra de la Virgen, is the Virgen del Castillo hermitage, built on the ruins of a castle of Arab origin.
Castle that is located right at the top of the town of Almadén and that is also called the Ermita Virgen del Castillo. The mountain has the same name as the castle.
